mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals peak labor demand and impending corporate margin compression, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth falls below 50k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and retail sales revenue confirms broad-based consumer spending momentum, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the market baseline when housing and retail data diverge.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, forcing equity risk premiums higher and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains above 0.05 over any 3-year period, or the signal fails to predict market declines during historical supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate hikes and accelerating consumer price inflation compress real yields and trigger equity risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% in 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window, or forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous rate hikes and inflation acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations where GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms labor market tightness without overheating, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k in 3 months while UNRATE remains below 5.5%
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when payroll growth exceeds 100k but unemployment rises.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production capacity signals improving corporate operating leverage and margin stability, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
